1	------------------------------- MODULE TLAPS --------------------------------
2	
3	(* Backend pragmas. *)
4	
5	
6	(***************************************************************************)
7	(* Each of these pragmas can be cited with a BY or a USE.  The pragma that *)
8	(* is added to the context of an obligation most recently is the one whose *)
9	(* effects are triggered.                                                  *)
10	(***************************************************************************)
11	
12	(***************************************************************************)
13	(* The following pragmas should be used only as a last resource.  They are *)
14	(* dependent upon the particular backend provers, and are unlikely to have *)
15	(* any effect if the set of backend provers changes.  Moreover, they are   *)
16	(* meaningless to a reader of the proof.                                   *)
17	(***************************************************************************)
18	
19	
20	(**************************************************************************)
21	(* Backend pragma: use the SMT solver for arithmetic.                     *)
22	(*                                                                        *)
23	(* This method exists under this name for historical reasons.             *)
24	(**************************************************************************)
25	
26	SimpleArithmetic == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3") }*)
27	
28	
29	(**************************************************************************)
30	(* Backend pragma: SMT solver                                             *)
31	(*                                                                        *)
32	(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2. The supported  *)
33	(* fragment includes first-order logic, set theory, functions and         *)
34	(* records.                                                               *)
35	(* SMT calls the smt-solver with the default timeout of 5 seconds         *)
36	(* while SMTT(n) calls the smt-solver with a timeout of n seconds.        *)
37	(**************************************************************************)
38	
39	SMT == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3") }*)
40	SMTT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@) }*)
41	
42	
43	(**************************************************************************)
44	(* Backend pragma: CVC4 SMT solver                                        *)
45	(*                                                                        *)
46	(* These methods translate the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and call CVC4. *)
47	(**************************************************************************)
48	
49	(* The CVC3* methods are here for backward compatibility. They call CVC4. *)
50	CVC3 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "cvc33") }*)
51	CVC3T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@) }*)
52	
53	CVC4 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "cvc33") }*)
54	CVC4T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@) }*)
55	
56	
57	(**************************************************************************)
58	(* Backend pragma: Yices SMT solver                                       *)
59	(*                                                                        *)
60	(* This method translates the proof obligation to Yices native language.  *)
61	(**************************************************************************)
62	
63	Yices == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "yices3") }*)
64	YicesT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@) }*)
65	
66	(**************************************************************************)
67	(* Backend pragma: veriT SMT solver                                       *)
68	(*                                                                        *)
69	(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and calls veriT.*)
70	(**************************************************************************)
71	
72	veriT == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "verit") }*)
73	veriTT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@) }*)
74	
75	(**************************************************************************)
76	(* Backend pragma: Zipperposition solver                                  *)
77	(*                                                                        *)
78	(* This method translates the proof obligation to TPTP and                *)
79	(* calls Zipperposition.                                                  *)
80	(**************************************************************************)
81	
82	Zipper == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "zipper") }*)
83	ZipperT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zipper"; timeout:@) }*)
84	
85	(**************************************************************************)
86	(* Backend pragma: Z3 SMT solver                                          *)
87	(*                                                                        *)
88	(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and calls Z3.   *)
89	(* Z3 is used by default but you can also explicitly call it.             *)
90	(**************************************************************************)
91	
92	Z3 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "z33") }*)
93	Z3T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@) }*)
94	
95	(**************************************************************************)
96	(* Backend pragma: SPASS superposition prover                             *)
97	(*                                                                        *)
98	(* This method translates the proof obligation to the DFG format language *)
99	(* supported by the ATP SPASS. The translation is based on the SMT one.   *)
100	(**************************************************************************)
101	
102	Spass == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "spass") }*)
103	SpassT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"spass"; timeout:@) }*)
104	
105	(**************************************************************************)
106	(* Backend pragma: The PTL propositional linear time temporal logic       *)
107	(* prover.  It currently is the LS4 backend.                              *)
108	(*                                                                        *)
109	(* This method translates the negetation of the proof obligation to       *)
110	(* Seperated Normal Form (TRP++ format) and checks for unsatisfiability   *)
111	(**************************************************************************)
112	
113	LS4 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4") }*)
114	LS4T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4"; timeout:@) }*)
115	PTL == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4") }*)
116	
117	(**************************************************************************)
118	(* Backend pragma: Zenon with different timeouts (default is 10 seconds)  *)
119	(*                                                                        *)
120	(**************************************************************************)
121	
122	Zenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon") }*)
123	ZenonT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:@) }*)
124	
125	(********************************************************************)
126	(* Backend pragma: Isabelle with different timeouts and tactics     *)
127	(*  (default is 30 seconds/auto)                                    *)
128	(********************************************************************)
129	
130	Isa == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle") }*)
131	IsaT(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; timeout:@) }*)
132	IsaM(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@) }*)
133	IsaMT(X,Y) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@; timeout:@) }*)
134	
135	(***************************************************************************)
136	(* The following theorem expresses the (useful implication of the) law of  *)
137	(* set extensionality, which can be written as                             *)
138	(*                                                                         *)
139	(*    THEOREM  \A S, T : (S = T) <=> (\A x : (x \in S) <=> (x \in T))      *)
140	(*                                                                         *)
141	(* Theorem SetExtensionality is sometimes required by the SMT backend for  *)
142	(* reasoning about sets. It is usually counterproductive to include        *)
143	(* theorem SetExtensionality in a BY clause for the Zenon or Isabelle      *)
144	(* backends. Instead, use the pragma IsaWithSetExtensionality to instruct  *)
145	(* the Isabelle backend to use the rule of set extensionality.             *)
146	(***************************************************************************)
147	IsaWithSetExtensionality == TRUE
148	           (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")}*)
149	
150	THEOREM SetExtensionality == \A S,T : (\A x : x \in S <=> x \in T) => S = T
151	OBVIOUS
152	
153	(***************************************************************************)
154	(* The following theorem is needed to deduce NotInSetS \notin SetS from    *)
155	(* the definition                                                          *)
156	(*                                                                         *)
157	(*   NotInSetS == CHOOSE v : v \notin SetS                                 *)
158	(***************************************************************************)
159	THEOREM NoSetContainsEverything == \A S : \E x : x \notin S
160	OBVIOUS (*{by (isabelle "(auto intro: inIrrefl)")}*)
161	-----------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	
163	
164	
165	(********************************************************************)
166	(********************************************************************)
167	(********************************************************************)
168	
169	
170	(********************************************************************)
171	(* Old versions of Zenon and Isabelle pragmas below                 *)
172	(* (kept for compatibility)                                         *)
173	(********************************************************************)
174	
175	
176	(**************************************************************************)
177	(* Backend pragma: Zenon with different timeouts (default is 10 seconds)  *)
178	(*                                                                        *)
179	(**************************************************************************)
180	
181	SlowZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:20) }*)
182	SlowerZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:40) }*)
183	VerySlowZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:80) }*)
184	SlowestZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:160) }*)
185	
186	
187	
188	(********************************************************************)
189	(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's automatic search ("auto")             *)
190	(*                                                                  *)
191	(* This pragma bypasses Zenon. It is useful in situations involving *)
192	(* essentially simplification and equational reasoning.             *)
193	(* Default imeout for all isabelle tactics is 30 seconds.           *)
194	(********************************************************************)
195	Auto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto") }*)
196	SlowAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:120) }*)
197	SlowerAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:480) }*)
198	SlowestAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:960) }*)
199	
200	(********************************************************************)
201	(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's "force" tactic                        *)
202	(*                                                                  *)
203	(* This pragma bypasses Zenon. It is useful in situations involving *)
204	(* quantifier reasoning.                                            *)
205	(********************************************************************)
206	Force == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force") }*)
207	SlowForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:120) }*)
208	SlowerForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:480) }*)
209	SlowestForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:960) }*)
210	
211	(***********************************************************************)
212	(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's "simplification" tactics                 *)
213	(*                                                                     *)
214	(* These tactics simplify the goal before running one of the automated *)
215	(* tactics. They are often necessary for obligations involving record  *)
216	(* or tuple projections. Use the SimplfyAndSolve tactic unless you're  *)
217	(* sure you can get away with just Simplification                      *)
218	(***********************************************************************)
219	SimplifyAndSolve        == TRUE
220	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?") }*)
221	SlowSimplifyAndSolve    == TRUE
222	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:120) }*)
223	SlowerSimplifyAndSolve  == TRUE
224	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:480) }*)
225	SlowestSimplifyAndSolve == TRUE
226	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:960) }*)
227	
228	Simplification == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp") }*)
229	SlowSimplification == TRUE
230	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:120) }*)
231	SlowerSimplification  == TRUE
232	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:480) }*)
233	SlowestSimplification == TRUE
234	    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:960) }*)
235	
236	(**************************************************************************)
237	(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's tableau prover ("blast")                    *)
238	(*                                                                        *)
239	(* This pragma bypasses Zenon and uses Isabelle's built-in theorem        *)
240	(* prover, Blast. It is almost never better than Zenon by itself, but     *)
241	(* becomes very useful in combination with the Auto pragma above. The     *)
242	(* AutoBlast pragma first attempts Auto and then uses Blast to prove what *)
243	(* Auto could not prove. (There is currently no way to use Zenon on the   *)
244	(* results left over from Auto.)                                          *)
245	(**************************************************************************)
246	Blast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast") }*)
247	SlowBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:120) }*)
248	SlowerBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:480) }*)
249	SlowestBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:960) }*)
250	
251	AutoBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast") }*)
252	
253	
254	(**************************************************************************)
255	(* Backend pragmas: multi-back-ends                                       *)
256	(*                                                                        *)
257	(* These pragmas just run a bunch of back-ends one after the other in the *)
258	(* hope that one will succeed. This saves time and effort for the user at *)
259	(* the expense of computation time.                                       *)
260	(**************************************************************************)
261	
262	(* CVC3 goes first because it's bundled with TLAPS, then the other SMT
263	   solvers are unlikely to succeed if CVC3 fails, so we run zenon and
264	   Isabelle before them. *)
265	AllProvers == TRUE (*{
266	    by (prover:"cvc33")
267	    by (prover:"zenon")
268	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto")
269	    by (prover:"spass")
270	    by (prover:"smt3")
271	    by (prover:"yices3")
272	    by (prover:"verit")
273	    by (prover:"z33")
274	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force")
275	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")
276	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?")
277	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp")
278	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast")
279	  }*)
280	AllProversT(X) == TRUE (*{
281	    by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@)
282	    by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:@)
283	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:@)
284	    by (prover:"spass"; timeout:@)
285	    by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@)
286	    by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@)
287	    by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@)
288	    by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@)
289	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:@)
290	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)"; timeout:@)
291	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:@)
292	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:@)
293	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast"; timeout:@)
294	  }*)
295	
296	AllSMT == TRUE (*{
297	    by (prover:"cvc33")
298	    by (prover:"smt3")
299	    by (prover:"yices3")
300	    by (prover:"verit")
301	    by (prover:"z33")
302	  }*)
303	AllSMTT(X) == TRUE (*{
304	    by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@)
305	    by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@)
306	    by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@)
307	    by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@)
308	    by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@)
309	  }*)
310	
311	AllIsa == TRUE (*{
312	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto")
313	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force")
314	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")
315	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?")
316	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp")
317	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast")
318	  }*)
319	AllIsaT(X) == TRUE (*{
320	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:@)
321	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:@)
322	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)"; timeout:@)
323	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:@)
324	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:@)
325	    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast"; timeout:@)
326	  }*)
327	
328	
329	(**************************************************************************)
330	(* The pragma ExpandEnabled invokes expansion of the operator ENABLED.    *)
331	(*                                                                        *)
332	(* The pragma ExpandCdot invokes expansion of the operator \cdot.         *)
333	(*                                                                        *)
334	(* The pragma AutoUSE invokes automated expansion of definitions,         *)
335	(* for both of ExpandEnabled and ExpandCdot, when each is present.        *)
336	(*                                                                        *)
337	(* The pragma Lambdify invokes expansion of the operators                 *)
338	(* ENABLED and \cdot to an intermediate form with bound VARIABLES,        *)
339	(* which is a form before introducing rigid quantifiers.                  *)
340	(* The pragma Lambdify is sound for occurrences of ENABLED and \cdot      *)
341	(* that are not nested.                                                   *)
342	(**************************************************************************)
343	ExpandENABLED == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"expandenabled") }*)
344	ExpandCdot == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"expandcdot") }*)
345	AutoUSE == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"autouse") }*)
346	Lambdify == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"lambdify") }*)
347	ENABLEDaxioms == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"enabledaxioms") }*)
348	LevelComparison == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"levelcomparison") }*)
349	
350	(* The operators EnabledWrapper and CdotWrapper occur in an intermediate  *)
351	(* representation within TLAPM.                                           *)
352	EnabledWrapper(Op(_)) == FALSE
353	CdotWrapper(Op(_)) == FALSE
354	
355	(***************************************************************************)
356	(* The following may be used in a `BY ONLY ThmName` for unit testing the   *)
357	(* triviality checks in TLAPM.                                             *)
358	(***************************************************************************)
359	Trivial == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"trivial") }*)
360	
361	
362	=============================================================================
363	
364	The material below is obsolete: the TLA proof rules below are superseded by
365	the PTL decision procedure, and their formulation is unsound for the semantics
366	of temporal reasoning that TLAPS adopts.
367	
368	----------------------------------------------------------------------------
369	(***************************************************************************)
370	(*                           TEMPORAL LOGIC                                *)
371	(*                                                                         *)
372	(* The following rules are intended to be used when TLAPS handles temporal *)
373	(* logic.  They will not work now.  Moreover when temporal reasoning is    *)
374	(* implemented, these rules may be changed or omitted, and additional      *)
375	(* rules will probably be added.  However, they are included mainly so     *)
376	(* their names will be defined, preventing the use of identifiers that are *)
377	(* likely to produce name clashes with future versions of this module.     *)
378	(***************************************************************************)
379	
380	
381	(***************************************************************************)
382	(* The following proof rules (and their names) are from the paper "The     *)
383	(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
384	(***************************************************************************)
385	THEOREM RuleTLA1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f,
386	                           P /\ (f' = f) => P'
387	                    PROVE  []P <=> P /\ [][P => P']_f
388	
389	THEOREM RuleTLA2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, STATE g,
390	                           ACTION A, ACTION B,
391	                           P /\ [A]_f => Q /\ [B]_g
392	                    PROVE  []P /\ [][A]_f => []Q /\ [][B]_g
393	
394	THEOREM RuleINV1 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE F,  ACTION N,
395	                           I /\ [N]_F => I'
396	                    PROVE  I /\ [][N]_F => []I
397	
398	THEOREM RuleINV2 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, ACTION N
399	                    PROVE  []I => ([][N]_f <=> [][N /\ I /\ I']_f)
400	
401	THEOREM RuleWF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, ACTION N, ACTION A,
402	                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
403	                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
404	                          P => ENABLED <<A>>_f
405	                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) => (P ~> Q)
406	
407	THEOREM RuleSF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f,
408	                          ACTION N, ACTION A, TEMPORAL F,
409	                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
410	                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
411	                          []P /\ [][N]_f /\ []F => <> ENABLED <<A>>_f
412	                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => (P ~> Q)
413	
414	(***************************************************************************)
415	(* The rules WF2 and SF2 in "The Temporal Logic of Actions" are obtained   *)
416	(* from the following two rules by the following substitutions: `.         *)
417	(*                                                                         *)
418	(*          ___        ___         _______________                         *)
419	(*      M <- M ,   g <- g ,  EM <- ENABLED <<M>>_g       .'                *)
420	(***************************************************************************)
421	THEOREM RuleWF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
422	                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
423	                          TEMPORAL F,
424	                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
425	                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
426	                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
427	                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F /\ <>[]EM => <>[]P
428	                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ []<>(~EM)
429	
430	THEOREM RuleSF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
431	                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
432	                          TEMPORAL F,
433	                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
434	                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
435	                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
436	                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F /\ []<>EM => <>[]P
437	                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ <>[](~EM)
438	
439	
440	(***************************************************************************)
441	(* The following rule is a special case of the general temporal logic      *)
442	(* proof rule STL4 from the paper "The Temporal Logic of Actions".  The    *)
443	(* general rule is for arbitrary temporal formulas F and G, but it cannot  *)
444	(* yet be handled by TLAPS.                                                *)
445	(***************************************************************************)
446	THEOREM RuleInvImplication ==
447	  ASSUME STATE F, STATE G,
448	         F => G
449	  PROVE  []F => []G
450	PROOF OMITTED
451	
452	(***************************************************************************)
453	(* The following rule is a special case of rule TLA2 from the paper "The   *)
454	(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
455	(***************************************************************************)
456	THEOREM RuleStepSimulation ==
457	  ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, STATE g,
458	         ACTION M, ACTION N,
459	         I /\ I' /\ [M]_f => [N]_g
460	  PROVE  []I /\ [][M]_f => [][N]_g
461	PROOF OMITTED
462	
463	(***************************************************************************)
464	(* The following may be used to invoke a decision procedure for            *)

------------------------------- MODULE TLAPS --------------------------------

(* Backend pragmas. *)


(***************************************************************************)
(* Each of these pragmas can be cited with a BY or a USE.  The pragma that *)
(* is added to the context of an obligation most recently is the one whose *)
(* effects are triggered.                                                  *)
(***************************************************************************)

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following pragmas should be used only as a last resource.  They are *)
(* dependent upon the particular backend provers, and are unlikely to have *)
(* any effect if the set of backend provers changes.  Moreover, they are   *)
(* meaningless to a reader of the proof.                                   *)
(***************************************************************************)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: use the SMT solver for arithmetic.                     *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method exists under this name for historical reasons.             *)
(**************************************************************************)

SimpleArithmetic == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3") }*)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: SMT solver                                             *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2. The supported  *)
(* fragment includes first-order logic, set theory, functions and         *)
(* records.                                                               *)
(* SMT calls the smt-solver with the default timeout of 5 seconds         *)
(* while SMTT(n) calls the smt-solver with a timeout of n seconds.        *)
(**************************************************************************)

SMT == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3") }*)
SMTT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@) }*)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: CVC4 SMT solver                                        *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* These methods translate the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and call CVC4. *)
(**************************************************************************)

(* The CVC3* methods are here for backward compatibility. They call CVC4. *)
CVC3 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "cvc33") }*)
CVC3T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@) }*)

CVC4 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "cvc33") }*)
CVC4T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@) }*)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Yices SMT solver                                       *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to Yices native language.  *)
(**************************************************************************)

Yices == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "yices3") }*)
YicesT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: veriT SMT solver                                       *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and calls veriT.*)
(**************************************************************************)

veriT == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "verit") }*)
veriTT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Zipperposition solver                                  *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to TPTP and                *)
(* calls Zipperposition.                                                  *)
(**************************************************************************)

Zipper == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "zipper") }*)
ZipperT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zipper"; timeout:@) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Z3 SMT solver                                          *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to SMTLIB2 and calls Z3.   *)
(* Z3 is used by default but you can also explicitly call it.             *)
(**************************************************************************)

Z3 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "z33") }*)
Z3T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: SPASS superposition prover                             *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the proof obligation to the DFG format language *)
(* supported by the ATP SPASS. The translation is based on the SMT one.   *)
(**************************************************************************)

Spass == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "spass") }*)
SpassT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"spass"; timeout:@) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: The PTL propositional linear time temporal logic       *)
(* prover.  It currently is the LS4 backend.                              *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This method translates the negetation of the proof obligation to       *)
(* Seperated Normal Form (TRP++ format) and checks for unsatisfiability   *)
(**************************************************************************)

LS4 == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4") }*)
LS4T(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4"; timeout:@) }*)
PTL == TRUE (*{ by (prover: "ls4") }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Zenon with different timeouts (default is 10 seconds)  *)
(*                                                                        *)
(**************************************************************************)

Zenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon") }*)
ZenonT(X) == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:@) }*)

(********************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Isabelle with different timeouts and tactics     *)
(*  (default is 30 seconds/auto)                                    *)
(********************************************************************)

Isa == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle") }*)
IsaT(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; timeout:@) }*)
IsaM(X) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@) }*)
IsaMT(X,Y) ==  TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:@; timeout:@) }*)

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following theorem expresses the (useful implication of the) law of  *)
(* set extensionality, which can be written as                             *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*    THEOREM  \A S, T : (S = T) <=> (\A x : (x \in S) <=> (x \in T))      *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* Theorem SetExtensionality is sometimes required by the SMT backend for  *)
(* reasoning about sets. It is usually counterproductive to include        *)
(* theorem SetExtensionality in a BY clause for the Zenon or Isabelle      *)
(* backends. Instead, use the pragma IsaWithSetExtensionality to instruct  *)
(* the Isabelle backend to use the rule of set extensionality.             *)
(***************************************************************************)
IsaWithSetExtensionality == TRUE
           (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")}*)

THEOREM SetExtensionality == \A S,T : (\A x : x \in S <=> x \in T) => S = T
OBVIOUS

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following theorem is needed to deduce NotInSetS \notin SetS from    *)
(* the definition                                                          *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*   NotInSetS == CHOOSE v : v \notin SetS                                 *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM NoSetContainsEverything == \A S : \E x : x \notin S
OBVIOUS (*{by (isabelle "(auto intro: inIrrefl)")}*)
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------



(********************************************************************)
(********************************************************************)
(********************************************************************)


(********************************************************************)
(* Old versions of Zenon and Isabelle pragmas below                 *)
(* (kept for compatibility)                                         *)
(********************************************************************)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Zenon with different timeouts (default is 10 seconds)  *)
(*                                                                        *)
(**************************************************************************)

SlowZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:20) }*)
SlowerZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:40) }*)
VerySlowZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:80) }*)
SlowestZenon == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:160) }*)



(********************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's automatic search ("auto")             *)
(*                                                                  *)
(* This pragma bypasses Zenon. It is useful in situations involving *)
(* essentially simplification and equational reasoning.             *)
(* Default imeout for all isabelle tactics is 30 seconds.           *)
(********************************************************************)
Auto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto") }*)
SlowAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:120) }*)
SlowerAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:480) }*)
SlowestAuto == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:960) }*)

(********************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's "force" tactic                        *)
(*                                                                  *)
(* This pragma bypasses Zenon. It is useful in situations involving *)
(* quantifier reasoning.                                            *)
(********************************************************************)
Force == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force") }*)
SlowForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:120) }*)
SlowerForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:480) }*)
SlowestForce == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:960) }*)

(***********************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's "simplification" tactics                 *)
(*                                                                     *)
(* These tactics simplify the goal before running one of the automated *)
(* tactics. They are often necessary for obligations involving record  *)
(* or tuple projections. Use the SimplfyAndSolve tactic unless you're  *)
(* sure you can get away with just Simplification                      *)
(***********************************************************************)
SimplifyAndSolve        == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?") }*)
SlowSimplifyAndSolve    == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:120) }*)
SlowerSimplifyAndSolve  == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:480) }*)
SlowestSimplifyAndSolve == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:960) }*)

Simplification == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp") }*)
SlowSimplification == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:120) }*)
SlowerSimplification  == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:480) }*)
SlowestSimplification == TRUE
    (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:960) }*)

(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragma: Isabelle's tableau prover ("blast")                    *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* This pragma bypasses Zenon and uses Isabelle's built-in theorem        *)
(* prover, Blast. It is almost never better than Zenon by itself, but     *)
(* becomes very useful in combination with the Auto pragma above. The     *)
(* AutoBlast pragma first attempts Auto and then uses Blast to prove what *)
(* Auto could not prove. (There is currently no way to use Zenon on the   *)
(* results left over from Auto.)                                          *)
(**************************************************************************)
Blast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast") }*)
SlowBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:120) }*)
SlowerBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:480) }*)
SlowestBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"blast"; timeout:960) }*)

AutoBlast == TRUE (*{ by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast") }*)


(**************************************************************************)
(* Backend pragmas: multi-back-ends                                       *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* These pragmas just run a bunch of back-ends one after the other in the *)
(* hope that one will succeed. This saves time and effort for the user at *)
(* the expense of computation time.                                       *)
(**************************************************************************)

(* CVC3 goes first because it's bundled with TLAPS, then the other SMT
   solvers are unlikely to succeed if CVC3 fails, so we run zenon and
   Isabelle before them. *)
AllProvers == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"cvc33")
    by (prover:"zenon")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto")
    by (prover:"spass")
    by (prover:"smt3")
    by (prover:"yices3")
    by (prover:"verit")
    by (prover:"z33")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast")
  }*)
AllProversT(X) == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"zenon"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"spass"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast"; timeout:@)
  }*)

AllSMT == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"cvc33")
    by (prover:"smt3")
    by (prover:"yices3")
    by (prover:"verit")
    by (prover:"z33")
  }*)
AllSMTT(X) == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"cvc33"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"smt3"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"yices3"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"verit"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"z33"; timeout:@)
  }*)

AllIsa == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp")
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast")
  }*)
AllIsaT(X) == TRUE (*{
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"force"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"(auto intro: setEqualI)"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp auto?"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"clarsimp"; timeout:@)
    by (prover:"isabelle"; tactic:"auto, blast"; timeout:@)
  }*)


(**************************************************************************)
(* The pragma ExpandEnabled invokes expansion of the operator ENABLED.    *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* The pragma ExpandCdot invokes expansion of the operator \cdot.         *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* The pragma AutoUSE invokes automated expansion of definitions,         *)
(* for both of ExpandEnabled and ExpandCdot, when each is present.        *)
(*                                                                        *)
(* The pragma Lambdify invokes expansion of the operators                 *)
(* ENABLED and \cdot to an intermediate form with bound VARIABLES,        *)
(* which is a form before introducing rigid quantifiers.                  *)
(* The pragma Lambdify is sound for occurrences of ENABLED and \cdot      *)
(* that are not nested.                                                   *)
(**************************************************************************)
ExpandENABLED == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"expandenabled") }*)
ExpandCdot == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"expandcdot") }*)
AutoUSE == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"autouse") }*)
Lambdify == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"lambdify") }*)
ENABLEDaxioms == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"enabledaxioms") }*)
LevelComparison == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"levelcomparison") }*)

(* The operators EnabledWrapper and CdotWrapper occur in an intermediate  *)
(* representation within TLAPM.                                           *)
EnabledWrapper(Op(_)) == FALSE
CdotWrapper(Op(_)) == FALSE

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following may be used in a `BY ONLY ThmName` for unit testing the   *)
(* triviality checks in TLAPM.                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
Trivial == TRUE  (*{ by (prover:"trivial") }*)


=============================================================================

The material below is obsolete: the TLA proof rules below are superseded by
the PTL decision procedure, and their formulation is unsound for the semantics
of temporal reasoning that TLAPS adopts.

----------------------------------------------------------------------------
(***************************************************************************)
(*                           TEMPORAL LOGIC                                *)
(*                                                                         *)
(* The following rules are intended to be used when TLAPS handles temporal *)
(* logic.  They will not work now.  Moreover when temporal reasoning is    *)
(* implemented, these rules may be changed or omitted, and additional      *)
(* rules will probably be added.  However, they are included mainly so     *)
(* their names will be defined, preventing the use of identifiers that are *)
(* likely to produce name clashes with future versions of this module.     *)
(***************************************************************************)


(***************************************************************************)
(* The following proof rules (and their names) are from the paper "The     *)
(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleTLA1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f,
                           P /\ (f' = f) => P'
                    PROVE  []P <=> P /\ [][P => P']_f

THEOREM RuleTLA2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, STATE g,
                           ACTION A, ACTION B,
                           P /\ [A]_f => Q /\ [B]_g
                    PROVE  []P /\ [][A]_f => []Q /\ [][B]_g

THEOREM RuleINV1 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE F,  ACTION N,
                           I /\ [N]_F => I'
                    PROVE  I /\ [][N]_F => []I

THEOREM RuleINV2 == ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, ACTION N
                    PROVE  []I => ([][N]_f <=> [][N /\ I /\ I']_f)

THEOREM RuleWF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f, ACTION N, ACTION A,
                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
                          P => ENABLED <<A>>_f
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) => (P ~> Q)

THEOREM RuleSF1 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE Q, STATE f,
                          ACTION N, ACTION A, TEMPORAL F,
                          P /\ [N]_f => (P' \/ Q'),
                          P /\ <<N /\ A>>_f => Q',
                          []P /\ [][N]_f /\ []F => <> ENABLED <<A>>_f
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => (P ~> Q)

(***************************************************************************)
(* The rules WF2 and SF2 in "The Temporal Logic of Actions" are obtained   *)
(* from the following two rules by the following substitutions: `.         *)
(*                                                                         *)
(*          ___        ___         _______________                         *)
(*      M <- M ,   g <- g ,  EM <- ENABLED <<M>>_g       .'                *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleWF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
                          TEMPORAL F,
                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F /\ <>[]EM => <>[]P
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ WF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ []<>(~EM)

THEOREM RuleSF2 == ASSUME STATE P, STATE f, STATE g, STATE EM,
                          ACTION A, ACTION B, ACTION N, ACTION M,
                          TEMPORAL F,
                          <<N /\ B>>_f => <<M>>_g,
                          P /\ P' /\ <<N /\ A>>_f /\ EM => B,
                          P /\ EM => ENABLED A,
                          [][N /\ ~B]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F /\ []<>EM => <>[]P
                   PROVE  [][N]_f /\ SF_f(A) /\ []F => []<><<M>>_g \/ <>[](~EM)


(***************************************************************************)
(* The following rule is a special case of the general temporal logic      *)
(* proof rule STL4 from the paper "The Temporal Logic of Actions".  The    *)
(* general rule is for arbitrary temporal formulas F and G, but it cannot  *)
(* yet be handled by TLAPS.                                                *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleInvImplication ==
  ASSUME STATE F, STATE G,
         F => G
  PROVE  []F => []G
PROOF OMITTED

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following rule is a special case of rule TLA2 from the paper "The   *)
(* Temporal Logic of Actions".                                             *)
(***************************************************************************)
THEOREM RuleStepSimulation ==
  ASSUME STATE I, STATE f, STATE g,
         ACTION M, ACTION N,
         I /\ I' /\ [M]_f => [N]_g
  PROVE  []I /\ [][M]_f => [][N]_g
PROOF OMITTED

(***************************************************************************)
(* The following may be used to invoke a decision procedure for            *)
(* propositional temporal logic.                                           *)
(***************************************************************************)
PropositionalTemporalLogic == TRUE
=============================================================================

input.content:
------------------------------- MODULE Record_SV_Spec -------------------------------

EXTENDS Naturals, TLAPS
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
CONSTANTS Participant

VARIABLES state

---------------------------------------------------------------------------
InitState == [maxBal |-> 0, maxVBal |-> 0]

Init == state = [p \in Participant |-> [q \in Participant |-> InitState]]

Prepare(p, b) ==
    /\ state[p][p].maxBal < b
    /\ state' = [state EXCEPT ![p][p].maxBal = b]
---------------------------------------------------------------------------
Next == \E p \in Participant, b \in Nat : Prepare(p, b)

Spec == Init /\ [][Next]_state
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

maxBal == [p \in Participant |-> state[p][p].maxBal]

SV == INSTANCE SimpleVoting

(*****************************************************************************)
(* Type invariant on `state`.                                                *)
(*****************************************************************************)
TypeOK == state \in [Participant -> [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]]

LEMMA InitTypeOK == Init => TypeOK
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME Init PROVE TypeOK OBVIOUS
  <1>1. InitState \in [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]
    BY DEF InitState
  <1>2. [q \in Participant |-> InitState] \in [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]
    BY <1>1
  <1>3. state = [p \in Participant |-> [q \in Participant |-> InitState]]
    BY DEF Init
  <1>4. state \in [Participant -> [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]]
    BY <1>2, <1>3
  <1> QED BY <1>4 DEF TypeOK

LEMMA NextTypeOK == TypeOK /\ [Next]_state => TypeOK'
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME TypeOK, [Next]_state PROVE TypeOK' OBVIOUS
  <1>1. CASE Next
    <2>1. PICK p \in Participant, b \in Nat : Prepare(p, b)
      BY <1>1 DEF Next
    <2>2. state' = [state EXCEPT ![p][p].maxBal = b]
      BY <2>1 DEF Prepare
    <2>3. state[p] \in [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]
      BY DEF TypeOK
    <2>4. state[p][p] \in [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]
      BY <2>3
    <2>5. [state[p][p] EXCEPT !.maxBal = b] \in [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]
      BY <2>4
    <2>6. [state[p] EXCEPT ![p] = [state[p][p] EXCEPT !.maxBal = b]] \in [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]
      BY <2>5, <2>3
    <2>7. state' = [state EXCEPT ![p] = [state[p] EXCEPT ![p] = [state[p][p] EXCEPT !.maxBal = b]]]
      BY <2>2
    <2>8. state' \in [Participant -> [Participant -> [maxBal: Nat, maxVBal: Nat]]]
      BY <2>6, <2>7 DEF TypeOK
    <2> QED BY <2>8 DEF TypeOK
  <1>2. CASE UNCHANGED state
    BY <1>2 DEF TypeOK
  <1> QED BY <1>1, <1>2

LEMMA InitImpl == Init => SV!Init
  <1> SUFFICES ASSUME Init PROVE SV!Init OBVIOUS
  <1>1. state = [p \in Participant |-> [q \in Participant |-> InitState]]
    BY DEF Init
  <1>2. \A p \in Participant : state[p][p] = InitState
    BY <1>1
  <1>3. \A p \in Participant : state[p][p].maxBal = 0
    BY <1>2 DEF InitState
  <1>4. maxBal = [p \in Participant |-> 0]
    BY <1>3 DEF maxBal
